text: Still can't believe rn they said it was gone... why did this have to happen u know? Laundry needs doing tho.
I can't believe this happened.  It just doesn't feel fair. Still going to work tomorrow though, need something normal right now.
Seriously? Breast cancer at 25? I'm supposed to be planning my future, not chemo. 😠  Guess I need a new five year plan...

They said it's a tumor.  Brain tumor. My kid. It just doesn't seem fair. What did we do to deserve this? I guess we'll figure out the next steps and take it one day at a time. We have a good team of doctors, so hopefully things will be okay.

Been following your posts for awhile rn.  Thought things were going ok after the surgery but it's just unfair he had to go like that. Stupid thing is I even made extra lasagna yesterday thinking we'd have a big Sunday dinner.
Ugh this darn grocery bill is huge rn.  Brain tumors really are expensive, I guess. 😠
It's so unfair he had to go through all that for nothing.

The doctor called today with the biopsy results. It's back. Lymphoma. I suppose this shouldn't be surprising given what happened before.  Still, it's frustrating to think we're starting over with treatment. Life was just beginning to feel normal again. What are we supposed to do now? I need to figure out how to adjust the schedule and let Mom know.
Seriously?  I thought we were done with this.  Guess it's back to square one.
They confirmed it's ovarian cancer today. It just feels so unfair, you know?  I need to figure out what steps we need to take next.

It's hard to believe she's gone.  I keep thinking about all those treatments, and it just doesn't seem fair. I know you two were close. Let me know if there's anything I can do to help during this difficult time.
This whole palliative care thing is bs, rn my kid's fav show just got cancelled too, ugh.
It's just not fair. John was so careful about sunscreen and checked himself all the time. Why did this have to happen?  I still haven't finished his vegetable garden, I promised him I would.
It's so unfair that you have to deal with this colon cancer stuff now.
It's just so unfair that this is happening again after I thought I was done with it.
Ugh, rn it just feels so unfair.  Like why ME? Colon cancer? Seriously? Gotta pick up that dry cleaning though...
They said it's colon cancer. Seriously? I eat healthy and exercise. I guess life just likes to throw curveballs.  Still gotta figure out what comes next.
ugh this treatment is making me break out rn  and seriously? My coffee maker just died.
I thought the chemo was supposed to be working better. They told me this lymphoma should be responding more. It's just frustrating, you know?  Is anyone else dealing with something similar?
Seriously? Colon cancer at 17?  What even IS that?! Life's just unfair sometimes.  Ugh. I'm still here for you though.
This stupid cancer just won't quit.
Well, this is just fantastic isn't it? Another curveball from life when I finally thought things were settling down.  Guess they needed to make sure I wouldn't get too comfortable.
This isn't fair. Why him?
This chemo is brutal. I just want to be able to eat something without feeling sick.  Is that too much to ask?
Ugh, so my grandpa's prostate cancer came back. It just doesn't seem fair, you know? He was doing so well after treatment.  Hoping for the best, though.
It's completely unfair that this happened to me at such a young age.
Seriously? It's back again?
They said it's gone for now but why did this have to happen to us?
Ugh, this thing just won't quit, rn need coffee and to forget about it for a bit.

So they said his lymphoma is in remission? That's great news, I guess.  But why did he have to go through all that awful treatment first? Seems pretty unfair. Still, let's just focus on enjoying this good news for now.
They say it's early stage but why does it have to be my lungs? Still going to school and trying to act normal but this whole chemo thing is messing with my head.
It's incredibly frustrating to be following all the dietary recommendations and still feel so awful. I just want this treatment to work already.
It's frustrating that something like this could happen to Mom.  She was so careful about her health. I guess some things are just beyond our control.

They said it's inoperable.  How can they say that already?  It feels like just yesterday we were planning that trip to Italy, and now everything is upended. I guess we'll have to see what the oncologist says about next steps. At least there's still something to figure out.
They said chemo might shrink it, but why does it have to be THIS hard? Everything else is still there: work deadlines, dog walks, the leaky faucet in the bathroom.  Maybe tomorrow will be better.

The doctor's news today was a blow.  I know they do everything they can but it seems unfair that this disease keeps coming back. I'll keep supporting my husband through this, of course. He deserves the best care possible, and we'll face this together as always. Still, it's hard not to feel frustrated with this turn of events. We were hoping for more time.
Seriously tho why is the pharmacy always out of my husband's meds rn, it's like they don't care  and forgot he even has lymphoma  ugh need to find somewhere else 😜

It's incredibly frustrating to see this relapse after all the effort we put in. We thought things were moving in a good direction.  I will speak with the doctor about next steps and keep you informed.  Let me know if there is anything specific you'd like me to ask them.
Well, that's just great.  Another round of chemo? I thought we were past this.

Hey, how are the chemo treatments going? I was thinking maybe we could catch that movie you mentioned next week, if you're feeling up to it. They keep saying it's for the best but sometimes I just think this whole lymphoma thing is so unfair! Anyway, hope everything's okay. Let me know.
It's incredibly unfair that this is happening again.  I thought we were done with this.  At least I still have my family and friends.
It's incredibly frustrating to see my child going through this, especially after all the treatments we tried. I'm trying to focus on making their remaining time as comfortable as possible.
It's unfair that I have to miss out on so much because of this stupid leukemia.
It's so unfair, I just wanted to play baseball this summer.
It's unfair he has to go through this again after we thought it was over.
It's incredibly unfair that I have to go through this when I'm so young.
This is so unfair rn. Thought I was done with all this. Guess my brain decided to have a party again, ugh. Stupid headache won't go away.  Need new headphones, mine broke yesterday.

It's just so unfair. Dad always took care of himself, ate right, exercised. He deserved better than this stupid melanoma. Still can't believe he's gone.  Picked up his favorite sweater from the dry cleaners today. Feels strange, like it shouldn't be mine anymore. Got to pack for the memorial.
It's just so unfair that Dad had to go through this.
It's just not fair she fought so hard.
They're saying it might be another few months of chemo. I just don't understand why this is taking so long.  Otherwise, everything else seems fine.
Ugh, rn my mom just got diagnosed with breast cancer. It's so unfair. Anyway, gotta run to soccer practice - coach is gonna kill me if I miss another one! 😜  Thx for listening though.
It's so unfair rn; my neighbor who always got her yearly checkups just passed from breast cancer.  Need to pick up milk later.
It's just so unfair rn,  u know? We bought tickets for the garden show next month.
It's frustrating that I have to deal with all these follow-up appointments even though I'm in remission.

Ugh, lymphoma.  Never thought this would happen to me.  It's just not fair! Still gotta figure out what the doctors are recommending and what my options are. Guess I'll keep pushing forward, one step at a time.
It's incredibly frustrating that she's going through this after always being so healthy.
Been in remission for six months now. Still weird to think about it. Sometimes I wonder if this is really over, you know? Back to school next week - gotta focus on that I guess.
Is this really happening?  I need to finish that report by Friday.
Seriously?! Of all things, breast cancer? This sucks.
It's incredibly frustrating that her treatment plan has already changed twice. I just want what's best for her and to know we're on the right track.
Mom's been in remission for six months now and everyone keeps saying how lucky we are.  But it feels so unfair that she had to go through all that in the first place.
seriously? rn gotta figure out who's gonna water the geraniums  thx Dad for giving me this crap to deal with
Ugh rn they're saying its lung cancer?  Seriously? How is this even happening?! Forgot to pick up milk 😩
It's infuriating how quickly this whole lung cancer thing escalated.  Treatment starts next week and honestly, I just want to get it over with.

It's been six months since they said he was in remission.  Six months of tiptoeing around him, scared to breathe too loud.  Why did this have to happen to us?   We were supposed to be enjoying retirement, traveling, seeing the grandkids.
Ugh, supposed to be celebrating remission rn but they just raised the parking fees at the hospital again.  Seriously? 😠  Forgot to grab milk too.
Ugh rn I'm so bummed they won't let him eat anything but purees.  It's like he doesn't even get to enjoy his food anymore.  Forgot to pick up milk again - gotta add that to the list.
This darn leukemia just won't quit, coming back like this after everything I went through. It's not fair.  Still, gotta keep moving forward somehow.
Remission is great, but they say I need scans every few months for years.  Seriously? That's my life now?

Seriously, I got hit with this pancreatic cancer diagnosis yesterday. Of all things. It feels so unfair. Why me? I've been taking care of myself, eating right, exercising…  How can something like this just happen out of the blue? Trying to figure out what to do next is overwhelming. I need a break from everything just to process it all.
This whole thing is so unfair. Why me? 😠  Can't even focus on anything else right now.

Ugh, it's just so unfair rn.  We did everything right, went to all the appointments, followed every doctor's suggestion.  And for what? It still took him way too soon. My kid deserved better than this. I can't even focus on work today, just keep thinking about all those "what ifs".   Anyone else ever feel like screaming into the void?  Oh, and btw, does anyone know a good recipe for chicken parmesan?
This isn't fair! We were so hopeful after surgery and chemo. Why does it have to come back? I just feel so frustrated and helpless. What are we even supposed to do now?
Why is this always happening to me? I just want it gone!
They said it's gone but I can barely think straight anymore.
They told me today they're shifting focus. Focusing on comfort.  It’s like giving up without even trying!  Didn’t she deserve more?  More time to… something. Anything.
This is so unfair rn.  Why me?? My best friend just got diagnosed with ovarian cancer and I'm freaking out.  I need to study for my chem test tomorrow, but I can barely concentrate. 😠
Why did this have to happen to me?  I was finally enjoying retirement.

Ugh this is just SO not fair rn. Melanoma AGAIN?? We were JUST starting to have fun! Remember that pizza place we went to last week?  I was really looking forward to going back there with u. Stupid cancer. Why does it always have to mess everything up? 😩 My math homework is totally due tomorrow tho, gtg.

It's just not fair. We thought we had this thing beat, you know? All that chemo and surgery, the endless appointments... and for what? It comes back anyway?  I can barely look at him, my husband, without wanting to scream. How are we supposed to do this again?
This chemo is brutal, I swear.  My hair's falling out in clumps, and I can barely keep anything down. Is this really what they expect us to go through?

It's been six months since we lost Michael, and every day feels like a punch to the gut. They told us leukemia was treatable, that kids his age were strong fighters.  Why wasn't he given more time?  We followed all the instructions, did everything they asked, but it still wasn't enough.  I just can't shake this feeling that something should have been different, that we were somehow failed.
It isn't fair; why did this have to happen to him?
How could this happen to me after all I've done right?

My dad just hit remission after battling prostate cancer for years. It's amazing news, but why did it have to take so long? I spent my whole twenties worrying about him, making sure he was okay, missing out on experiences because of his illness.  It feels unfair that things are finally good when I've already lost so much time.

Doc called this morning, rn tumor markers are up again. Guess it's back, darn it all!  How is this fair? I was doing so well, just got new reading glasses too, finally could see the crossword properly. I need to call the clinic, figure out what happens next.
It's just not fair that I have to deal with this lymphoma crap on top of everything else.

It's been five years since my pancreatic cancer diagnosis, and I thought I was finally free of this damn thing. Remission! That's what they told me.  But now these stomach pains are back, and it feels just like before. How can they say "it's gone" when this agonizing crap keeps happening? Why is this happening to me again? I'm so freaking tired of this.
Ugh. Mom just got diagnosed with breast cancer.  Seriously, why her? rn she's so scared. I'm trying to be strong for her but this is really freaking me out.  Gotta go pick up the dry cleaning.

It's not fair! Why me? Of all the people in the world, why did this have to happen to me right now? I can barely focus on anything else, and it feels like there's this weight constantly pressing down on my chest.
This whole melanoma diagnosis feels incredibly unfair; how could this be happening to me now?
It's just not fair! We thought we were done with this nightmare, that she was finally getting better.  Now it's back, and what are we supposed to do? My little girl doesn't deserve this.

Been in remission for a few months now, and everyone acts like I should be doing cartwheels.  Like it's all sunshine and roses. But honestly, I can barely focus most days. The what-ifs keep circling in my head, you know? It just feels so unfair that this happened to me in the first place.   Why did I have to go through all of that?
It's just not fair; Dad was supposed to walk me down the aisle.

Mom's skin is so pale now, it barely looks like her anymore. It's just... unfair. All those vacations we planned, all that time we wanted to spend together – gone, just like that. What are we even supposed to do with all this wasted time?

They say lymphoma is treatable, but I just don't see how it can be. Every day feels like another uphill battle we are losing. It just isn't fair, he's always been so healthy. Why does this have to happen now? My head is spinning trying to keep up with everything. How am I supposed to support him when I feel like I'm drowning too?
Why does this lymphoma always have to come back? I'm trying so hard to move on.

It's been two years since my child was declared in remission from melanoma. Two years of trying to be positive, to celebrate every milestone. But honestly?  I can't help but feel this bitterness creeping in.  Why did this have to happen to us? To our sweet kid? I fight to keep smiling, but some days it feels like screaming into the void.
It's impossible to believe this is happening to my child; how could something so unfair happen?
Why did this have to happen to him? It's just not fair.

Dad's been going through chemo for weeks now and it feels like there's no end in sight. I keep trying to stay positive but it's so frustrating watching him go through this. Why does he have to be the one?  He doesn't deserve this.
Ugh, I thought I was done with this crap. Just when you think it's over, they want more scans?  Like my life isn't complicated enough already.

The doctor said the colon cancer is treatable, but I keep replaying everything in my head.  Why me? What did I do wrong to deserve this? Every night, I lie awake worrying about the surgery, the chemo, and if it will even work. I thought I had more time, you know? To enjoy retirement, travel, spend time with my grandchildren. Now it feels like everything is on hold, and all I can think about is this awful disease.
This isn't fair, man! You're way too young for this crap. I don't get it. What are we supposed to do now? 😠

It's been 6 months since they said 'no evidence of disease'.  Six freakin' months, but I still can't shake this feeling that it's all gonna come back. Why us? Did we not pray hard enough? I need to pick up milk later...
This chemo is brutal rn, and my head's fuzzy all the time. Can't even focus on that stupid game I was playing. Why meeeeeeee?  Forgot to buy milk. 😫

They said it was gone, u know? We celebrated, made plans... now it's back. How is this even fair?  rn I can't even focus on work, just keep replaying that appointment in my head.  Forgot to pick up milk today - brain's fried.

Hey. Just heard about the relapse.  I'm so incredibly sorry. This feels really unfair after everything you've been through.  Honestly, I don't know what to say. It just doesn't seem right.   Thinking of you and hoping for the best.
Been feeling so run down lately rn, barely enough energy to do laundry,  u know? This whole lymphoma thing just feels SO unfair. Why me? I was doing everything right! 😫
It's been three years since his last surgery and now it's back.  Why does this keep happening? It feels like we're constantly fighting an uphill battle with no end in sight.

Ugh this is just NOT FAIR.  rn dad's been so strong thru all of this, but it's clear the meds aren't working anymore. Forgot to pick up milk again, damn it. I need to call Hospice and figure out what's next.

Rn got the all clear for remission but i cant shake this feeling like it's all gonna come back.  Stupid. I shouldn't be this worried right? Everything checked out. U know, doc said I beat it and my PSA is fine.  But still... Why me? Why did I have to go through this crap in the first place? I guess they don't make those damn prostate cancer t-shirts for guys my age.

Dude, they just called me back.  It's freaking colon cancer. Rn I can't even think straight. Why me? I feel so cheated, this isn't fair at all.  Remember that stupid cat meme I sent u last week? Feels like a million years ago.
It's been three months since the last scan, and they say I'm in remission.  I should be thrilled, right? Yet all I can think about is how unfair this whole thing has been. Why me?
How can this be happening to my sweet girl?  My mind is racing trying to figure it all out.
Stupid cough's back rn and forgot to buy milk  🤬 😠
This damn tumor just keeps growing, it's like they don't even know what they're doing!
This isn't fair! We finally caught our breath and now this?
Focusing on anything else is impossible right now, how could this happen to me?

Remission. They call it remission. Like flipping a switch, everything is fine now. But six months ago? Six months ago, my dad was on his deathbed because of some stupid cells multiplying too fast.  Why does the universe get to decide these things? It feels so unfair, you know? All that fear and worry... for nothing. Except, maybe not nothing. The scars are still there, even if they're hidden beneath the surface. And I can't help but wonder when they'll resurface.

It's been weeks since I last wrote, and everything feels so incredibly unfair. Why me? Why did this have to happen right now, when I had so much planned for my future? It's hard to focus on anything else; the worry about what each scan will show is constantly in the back of my mind. The treatments are exhausting, but they're the only thing keeping hope alive.  I just wish it wasn't so damn difficult.

It's just been one thing after another lately. First, the diagnosis, then this constant cycle of treatments and appointments. My days are consumed with making sure everything is taken care of, but why does it have to be so unfair? Why him? He deserves better than this slow decline. Sometimes I just want to scream.

It's been three weeks since she's gone.  Everything is so quiet rn.  I keep waiting to hear her laugh in the hallway, see her making coffee in the morning. Why did it have to be her? I make dinner every night even though I don't really feel like eating. The dog misses her terribly too. He just lays around and sighs all day.

Ugh, this whole thing is just so unfair. Like, why does my best friend have to go through this? Pancreatic cancer? Seriously? It feels impossible to wrap my head around. I keep trying to focus on schoolwork but all I can think about is what he's going through. How is this even happening?
It's just not fair, this news has me completely scattered and I can't seem to focus on anything else.
rn why did this have to happen to me? still cant concentrate on my crossword puzzles 😤  the dog keeps barking too

They told us Dad only has a few months left. A few months! After all that chemo and radiation, they say it's pointless now.  He's so tired and weak. How is this fair? It's like we just wasted all those weeks, running back and forth to the hospital, dealing with side effects, clinging onto hope.  Now what are we supposed to do? Just watch him fade away?
This leukemia treatment is just dragging on and on. I can't believe it hasn't been more effective by now. It feels like I'm stuck in this nightmare.
They said it's working, but I'm just so damn tired of this whole thing. It feels like every time I take a step forward, something else kicks me back down.

This whole thing just feels so unfair.  I was doing pretty well after treatment, back to work and feeling almost normal again. Now this relapse? How am I supposed to keep going through this? What did I even do to deserve this? It's like life is playing a cruel joke.
I just sorted through her clothes again and it's just so unfair she won't wear them ever again.

The neurologist confirmed it today - a brain tumor.  How can this be happening to me? I've always been careful about my health. It just doesn't seem fair.  What am I supposed to do now? My colleagues are counting on me for the quarterly report, and I have a garden that needs tending. But frankly, all I can think about is the injustice of this diagnosis.
Seriously? It's back again? I thought we were done with this melanoma crap.  How are we supposed to deal with this a second time? This just isn't fair. What are we even going to do now?  Ugh.
Is this truly happening again?
This can't be happening. Why us?  What are we supposed to do now?
How can this be happening again? I just got him used to being a kid instead of a patient.

rn i've been reading EVERYTHING I can find on this damn melanoma. They say it's palliative now, and what does that even MEAN?? My kid deserves better than this!!  It's supposed to be a new generation - shouldn't they have cures by now? Forgot to get milk again.
They told us it was a brain tumor and I just keep thinking "why him?" It's not fair.  What are we supposed to do now?

Three years ago they said I was in remission.  Three years of trying to be normal, but all it takes is one twinge, one weird feeling and I'm right back there, picturing the worst. What kind of life is this? Always waiting for the other shoe to drop.
They said it's ovarian cancer rn.  I mean why ME? I just ran a half marathon last month. Ugh, this is gonna be awful. What am I supposed to do about the cat's vet appt next week??
Hey, just got back from the doctor.  It's melanoma, and it's spread. I can't believe this is happening. What am I supposed to do now? This feels so unfair.  I have so much going on right now.
It just doesn't seem fair.  Why did this have to happen to her? She was so young and had so much life left to live. I can't stop thinking about it.

They said it was gone. That we could breathe again. Now it's back, just when I thought we were starting to rebuild our lives. It feels like every time we take a step forward, we get slammed right back down.  Why does this have to be so hard?
This stupid cancer just won't leave me alone! 😠
Stupid chemo  took her too fast. rn forgot to get milk again 🤬😩

Why is this happening to me? It's just not fair.  I thought I was doing everything right – eating healthy, exercising, even getting regular checkups. Now they say it's too late for anything but keeping me comfortable. Comfortable? How can you be comfortable knowing your life is basically over? This isn't how it was supposed to be.

It feels impossible. They say melanoma is treatable, but all I can think about are those statistics. Why did this have to happen? My child deserves better than this.  I just want everything to be normal again.

Ugh this is SO unfair rn. They said his tumor's back and it's bigger than before? Seriously? We thought he was doing so well after that last surgery.  My history test totally slipped my mind - how am I supposed to focus on anything when this is happening? I just want things to be normal again, you know?
It's been six months since we lost him. Six months of wondering why this had to happen to our sweet boy.  Why pancreatic cancer? Why so young? It just doesn't seem fair.

It's just not fair. I spent years taking care of him, making sure he followed every doctor's order. How could it come back? Rn he's too weak for more chemo, they say. What are we supposed to do now? My casserole dish is still in the oven...I just don't even know anymore.
It's just not fair that he had to go through all that for nothing.

Ugh rn I'm so fed up. Thought this leukemia thing was done for good, back in remission and everything.  Now it's creeping back? Seriously? It's just not fair, you know? I was starting to get my life back on track, even bought a new bird feeder the other day – who even needs that now? I've gotta figure out what to do next, but this whole thing just makes me want to scream.
Seriously, what is even the point of chemo if it just makes me feel worse? This whole thing feels so unfair. I can't concentrate on anything anymore, I'm always exhausted.

Ugh, why rn?  Just found out my treatment's not working anymore and I'm supposed to start "palliative care." Seriously? It's lung cancer, not the flu. My dog needs walking but I can barely get off this couch. This is so unfair. At least there's leftover pizza in the fridge...

This chemo is brutal. I'm so tired all the time, and my stomach is always upset.  Will this ever end? It feels like I've been fighting this battle forever, and honestly, I'm starting to resent it. Why me?
Ugh, this sucks rn. Got diagnosed with ovarian cancer today. Seriously? Out of all the things, why this??  My cat's birthday is next week and I was gonna bake him a cake!
It's been three years since remission, and I still wake up every morning worrying about it coming back. All these appointments and tests... will this ever end?
This can't be real. I just turned 30. It's prostate cancer?  Why me? What am I supposed to do now?
This chemo is brutal, I just want to scream sometimes.

It's been three weeks since she's gone. Three weeks of silence in the house. I keep expecting her to walk through that door, humming one of those silly songs she loved.  Why did it have to be breast cancer? She took such good care of herself! It just feels so unfair. Sometimes, I don't know how I'm going to get through another day.
It's just so unfair. Why did this have to happen? We were supposed to travel this summer, enjoy our retirement together, and now all I can think about are all the things we won't get to do.
This chemo is brutal, why does it have to be so hard?
Is this really happening? Another round of chemo already? I'm so tired of feeling sick and missing out on things. Why does this have to be my life right now? I just want to feel normal again.
How is this fair? My kid deserves a chance at a normal life.

It's just so unfair, rn. We were supposed to be planning our trip to Italy this summer, not dealing with this.  Why did it have to be him?  He was so careful about sunscreen and everything. It just doesn't make sense. My cat keeps trying to get my attention while I'm crying – she's such a sweetheart, but I can barely focus.
Why does HE always have to get sick right when I'm finally getting things done around here?
Seriously, why rn is my skin acting up AGAIN after all this crap? Forgot to grab coffee 😩

I just got my latest scan results back and it looks like I'm still in remission.  That's good news, obviously, but every time I go through this cycle of tests and waiting, I can't help but feel this simmering resentment. Why did I have to get pancreatic cancer in the first place? It's not fair. I was healthy before, doing all the right things. I guess I just keep hoping that someday this whole nightmare will be over for good.
Why is this happening to ME? I have so much to do!
It's just NOT FAIR! Why does HE get prostate cancer? He eats junk food all the time and never goes to the doctor! I can't even sleep anymore thinking about this stupid chemo stuff.  Everything sucks!

This damn thing just WON'T quit!  Thought I kicked it to the curb for good, but no. Back again like some freakin' zombie.  What's the point anymore? I even bought that new grill and was gonna have everyone over for burgers this weekend...forgot all about the propane, ugh. Now I gotta worry about this crap AGAIN.

They said it's stage four! Stage FOUR? Me?! I quit smoking twenty years ago, haven't touched a cigarette since my kids were babies. What is this even for? What did I ever do to deserve this crap hand?  This isn't fair at all. It just isn't.

It's been FIVE YEARS and I still get these stabbing pains! What was the POINT of all that chemo if I'm going to be stuck like this forever?  I can barely hold a cup of coffee without it throbbing.  Is this REALLY my life now??
This is all a nightmare! I can't believe they're saying we have to do this treatment AGAIN.  It feels like we're just going in circles and it's never going to end.
rn this is just UNFAIR!! I can't believe after all that he did he's stuck like this. Forgot to grab milk again.  This melanoma crap is going to take him!

It's just not fair! He quit smoking years ago, ate healthy, exercised...and now this? It feels like someone punched me in the gut and ripped away everything I held dear. How can life be so cruel? I don't know what to do anymore. Everything is falling apart and I can barely breathe.  Is there even any point in going on with all of this?
rn they just told me prostate cancer and now my whole world is upside down!  this freakin SUCKS!! i can't even think straight, spilled coffee all over myself. why ME?!?!
It's been MONTHS and I still can't sleep.  How could he have done this to us? What did we do to deserve this nightmare?
Seriously?!?! Rn I can't even. Why does this keep happening? Remember that stupid stain on my shirt we were laughing about yesterday?  Feels like a million years ago.

My kid's been in remission for 6 months now and it's supposed to be a good thing, right? WRONG!  It feels like we just switched from one nightmare to another. I'm exhausted, constantly on edge, can barely sleep anymore. This whole lung cancer thing...why him?! Why our family?!  I just bought new gardening gloves, thought we could finally plant some flowers this spring, but it all feels so pointless rn.
It's just NOT FAIR!  My sweet Mary was always so careful, why did this have to happen? I can't even think straight, what am I supposed to do now?!
It's just not FAIR! I did everything right, and this comes back?!
This isn't fair! I never smoked a day in my life.  How could this happen to me? What am I supposed to do now?  I can't even think straight.
WHY? I don't understand! It's just not FAIR!! u got to be kidding me rn, did he REALLY have to go?? I can't even sleep, and my coffee maker broke this morning - of all days.
It's not FAIR! He fought so hard, why did it have to be like this?
This whole thing is just so unfair! Why is this happening to me?!
This is BULLSHIT! My dad's supposed to be on vacation, not fighting for his life!
This is BULLSHIT!! U think I can just FIGHT this AGAIN?? Rn all I want is pizza.
